# Enrollment Requirements Checklist: UC-25 Enroll in All Courses from a Chosen Schedule Option

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, consistency, and measurability of UC-25 batch-enrollment requirements before implementation
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/025-branch-enroll-all/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for full success, partial success, atomic cancellation, and system-failure outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK002 Are linked section types such as lecture, lab, and tutorial explicitly included in the batch-enrollment scope? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec define what information the final enrollment summary must include for each section outcome? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK004 Are eligibility checks defined broadly enough to cover holds, prerequisites, and term rules before enrollment attempts begin? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the default partial-success policy stated unambiguously and distinguished from atomic enrollment policy? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK006 Is “safe partial success” defined clearly enough to understand when unaffected sections may still proceed? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK007 Is “consistent final enrollment state” defined clearly enough to distinguish allowed partial enrollment from rollback-required failure states? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK008 Is the meaning of section outcome statuses such as enrolled, rejected, blocked, and cancelled clear enough for objective validation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Key Entities]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are the policy rules in user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and assumptions consistent with each other? [Consistency, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Requirements, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK010 Do the success criteria use the same batch-outcome concepts as the functional requirements and user stories? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK011 Are the dependencies on enrollment-period status, holds/payment data, and course data consistent between the spec and the plan? [Consistency, Spec §Dependencies, Plan §Summary, Plan §Technical Context]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Can each success criterion be objectively verified without assuming unstated implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Are acceptance scenarios defined for both standard and failure-path batch-enrollment behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [x] CHK014 Is traceability to `UC-25` and `UC-25-AS` explicit at the story level and reinforced in the requirements? [Traceability, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-011]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are section-full, real-time-conflict, holds/prerequisites, system-error, and atomic-policy scenarios all covered in the written requirements? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK016 Are requirements clear that Enroll All applies to one selected schedule option only and does not affect outside sections? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK017 Does the spec define what happens when a selected schedule option contains a mix of enrollable and non-enrollable sections? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are between-generation and enrollment-time changes, such as newly full sections or new real-time conflicts, addressed explicitly? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK019 Are rollback expectations defined for system-error cases after tentative enrollment work has started? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK020 Are atomic-policy edge cases addressed distinctly from default partial-success behavior? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK021 Are performance expectations for final summary delivery stated with measurable timing criteria? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-001, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK022 Are accessibility requirements specified for summaries, failure reasons, and cancellation messages? [Completeness, Spec §NFR-002]
- [x] CHK023 Are privacy requirements defined so only the authenticated student's selected schedule option and results are accessible? [Completeness, Spec §NFR-003]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are assumptions about existing selected schedule options and out-of-scope waitlist behavior clearly separated from requirements? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK025 Are dependency requirements for enrollment, payment/holds, and course data availability documented with enough detail to validate outage or stale-data scenarios? [Dependency, Spec §Dependencies, Gap]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Is there any remaining ambiguity about when full-batch rollback is required versus when partial success is acceptable? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK027 Are any open issues from `UC-25.md` either resolved in the spec or intentionally excluded with clear wording? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Requirements]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Checklist review completed on 2026-03-11; resulting spec updates clarified per-section summary content, safe partial-success boundaries, consistency-preservation rules, and dependency-unavailability failure handling.
